# Candidate Management Procedures {#sec-cmps}

```{r}
#| include: false
library(MSEtool)
library(ggplot2)
rnorm(5)
```

Within the MSE framework, each candidate MP
is applied to the historical data simulated by each operating model and then used to set management advice
at each management cycle throughout the projection period. The performance of each MP is then compared across
the Reference and Robustness OMs (see @sec-oms-reference and @sec-oms-robustness) using the performance metrics
described in @sec-performance-metrics.

The candidate MPs described in this chapter are preliminary and intended to illustrate the range of approaches
available to the SALB MSE Technical Team, spanning simple constant catch rules through to methods that respond
to trends in an abundance index or to a full stock assessment model.

::: {.callout-note}
## Preliminary Candidate MPs
The MPs described below are preliminary and have been included to test the MSE framework. The final set of
candidate MPs will be developed and agreed with the SALB MSE Technical Team, and this chapter will be
updated accordingly.
:::

@tbl-cmps summarises the candidate MPs currently coded in the MSE framework.

::: {#tbl-cmps}
| MP | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `SP_FMSY` | Model-based | Sets the TAC to the catch expected to achieve MSY, based on a surplus production stock assessment model fitted to the simulated catch and index data. |
| `SP_75FMSY` | Model-based | As `SP_FMSY`, but sets the TAC to a more precautionary 75% of the level expected to achieve MSY. |
| `IRatio` | Index-based | Adjusts the TAC up or down depending on whether recent values of the abundance index are higher or lower than a historical reference period. |
| `ISlope` | Index-based | Adjusts the TAC up or down depending on the recent trend (slope) in the abundance index. |
| `MCC1` | Stepped, index-based | Adjusts a baseline TAC up or down through a series of pre-agreed steps, depending on the ratio of the recent abundance index to a historical reference period. |
| `MCC2` | Stepped, index-based | As `MCC1`, but starting from a slightly higher baseline TAC. |

Candidate management procedures currently coded in the MSE framework, grouped by general approach.
:::
